meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Bridge the experience gap. Learn to build high-converting user onboarding flows with Antigravity and Claude Code to showcase your skills to employers.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is a myth; you lack a showcase of technical empathy. By building a sophisticated, data-driven user onboarding flow, you demonstrate mastery over state management, UX psychology, and API orchestration—the exact trifecta senior engineers look for when hiring junior developers for complex SaaS environments.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio is the most daunting hurdle in a developer's career. You have the skills, but employers can't see past the lack of production-grade experience. The industry doesn't need more 'To-Do' apps; it needs engineers who understand how to guide a user from sign-up to 'Aha!' moment without friction.

By mastering user onboarding, you move from a coder to a product-focused builder. This project forces you to solve real-world problems: authentication state, progressive profiling, and feature discovery. Let’s turn your empty GitHub into a magnet for recruiters by building a feature that actually impacts retention.

steps.title: Architecting the State Machine
steps.description: Define the user journey stages. Onboarding is a series of state transitions. We need to map these out before writing a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust React state machine architecture for a 3-step onboarding flow including user profile collection, feature preference selection, and a final tutorial tour.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a state machine hook for onboarding steps in src/hooks/useOnboarding.ts'

steps.title: Building the Progressive UI
steps.description: Create a modular UI component that updates based on the current state, ensuring a seamless, non-blocking user experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive Tailwind CSS component for a stepper-based onboarding UI that supports smooth transitions between form fields.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate OnboardingStepper.tsx component with Tailwind classes'

steps.title: Implementing API Persistence
steps.description: Persist user progress so they can resume onboarding if they refresh the page. This shows you understand backend integration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure API integration handler that syncs current onboarding progress to a Supabase database using optimistic updates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create auth-protected service to save onboarding state to Supabase'

steps.title: Adding Contextual Analytics
steps.description: Track where users drop off. This is a pro-level feature that proves you think like a growth engineer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate a lightweight event tracking function for each onboarding step to identify user friction points.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Add event logging to useOnboarding hook to track step completion rates'

steps.title: Final QA and Edge-Case Handling
steps.description: Ensure the flow is bulletproof. Handle errors like network timeouts and invalid user inputs gracefully.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate comprehensive unit tests for the onboarding state machine to ensure all edge cases are covered.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Write Jest tests for the onboarding flow logic'

pro_tips: Never force a user to sign up before they see value; use a 'guest' state to allow trial before commitment.
Use 'optimistic UI' updates to make your onboarding feel instantaneous, even on slow connections.
Implement 'progressive profiling'—ask for only one piece of data per screen to keep conversion high.
Document your decision-making process in a README.md; employers hire based on your thought process, not just the code.
Add a 'Skip' button to every onboarding step; respecting user time is a mark of a senior developer.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to Vercel, record a 60-second Loom video explaining how you handled the state architecture, and link it prominently on your LinkedIn and GitHub profile.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' project and start building the one that gets you hired. You have the tools, the guide, and the technical edge. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
